title: Harriet Tubman's Final Freedom Mission
story: Harriet Tubman, the brave conductor of the Underground Railroad, completed her last daring mission in 1860. On December 28, she arrived safely in Auburn, New York, after years of secret journeys. Tubman had outsmarted slave catchers for eight long years, helping many people escape to freedom. Her courage and clever planning made her a true hero in the fight against slavery.

title: Galileo's Starry Mix-Up
story: Stargazers, get ready for a cosmic oops! In 1612, the famous astronomer Galileo peered through his telescope and spotted what he thought was a new 'fixed star' in the night sky. Little did he know, he had just become the first person to observe Neptune! This gas giant planet, hiding in plain sight, wouldn't be officially discovered as a planet for over 200 more years. On December 28, Galileo made his observation, unknowingly setting the stage for future astronomers to unravel the mysteries of our solar system.

title: Ancient Desert Survivor Discovered

On December 28, researchers confirmed its age, making it one of the oldest living organisms on Earth.

activity_question: What type of weather forecasts were first published on December 28, 1904?
follow_up_question: How do you think wireless forecasts changed people's daily lives compared to before?

title: San Francisco's Streetcar Revolution
story: Clang, clang! The streets of San Francisco buzzed with excitement in 1912. On December 28, the city's first public streetcars rolled out. These weren't just any trolleys - they belonged to the people! The San Francisco Municipal Railway, nicknamed MUNI, began its journey on Geary Street. It was a groundbreaking moment: the first city-owned streetcar system in America had arrived.
activity_question: What was special about San Francisco's new streetcars in 1912?
activity_choices: They were the first municipally owned streetcars
They were the fastest streetcars in the world
They could fly over traffic jams
activity_answer: They were the first municipally owned streetcars
follow_up_question: What does 'municipal' mean in this context?

title: The Return of the New Year Tree
story: Fir trees sparkled with joy! In 1935, Pavel Postyshev wrote a letter to Pravda newspaper, bringing back a beloved tradition. His words revived the New Year tree custom in the Soviet Union. On December 28, families read how decorating trees could spread cheer. Soon, glittering ornaments and twinkling lights returned to homes across the land.
activity_question: What tradition did Pavel Postyshev's letter revive?

title: Vanishing Flight: The DC-3 Mystery
story: A plane vanished into thin air! In 1948, a DC-3 airliner with the code NC16002 mysteriously disappeared. The aircraft was flying south of Miami, Florida when it suddenly went off the radar. Despite extensive searches, no trace of the plane or its passengers was ever found. This puzzling incident on December 28 remains one of aviation's unsolved mysteries.
activity_question: What type of aircraft disappeared in this story?

title: A Young Emperor Takes the Throne
story: Imagine ruling a country at just 11 years old! That's exactly what happened in 1308 when Emperor Hanazono of Japan took the throne. His reign marked an important chapter in Japanese history.
